rulings.Case Title: 399 FREMONT LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. CITY AND COUNTY CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014, Line 1, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is overruled as to the first cause of action. First, Petitioner's satisfaction of the Mitigation Fee Act requirements is not dispositive of whether Petitioner exhausted its administrative remedies. Rather, if administrative remedies are provided for Petitioner's protest, Petitioner must allege exhaustion thereof. However, it is not clear from either the face of the Complaint or the code provisions in Respondents' Request for Judicial Notice that the Building Code or Planning Code provide any administrative remedies for Petitioner's protest of the fee based on the grounds asserted in the Petition. Second, nothing in Respondents' Request for Judicial Notice conflicts with Petitioner's claim that the Fee Deferral Program was intended to establish a separate fee structure for those projects. Further, the language contained on the Fee Reports is not dispositive of whether the fee charged here was permissible under the Building and Planning Codes. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the causes of action for declaratory and injunctive relief as improper causes of action under the circumstances here. (See Zetterberg v. State Dept. of Public Health (1974) 43 Cal.App.3d 657, 663 ["A difference of opinion as to the interpretation of a statute as between a citizen and a governmental agency does not give rise to a justiciable controversy [for declaratory relief]."; Tejon Real Estate, LLC v. City of Los Angeles (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 149,154-55.) Additionally, Petitioner fails to allege or argue in opposition that its legal remedies are inadequate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE MARTINEZ VS. JOHN C. JEW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT JOHN JEW Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER IMPOSING TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND DISMISSES WITH PREJUDICE PLAINTIFF JOSE MARTINEZ'S COMPLAINT IN ITS ENTIRETY. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to MSchreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA REBOLLEDO et al VS. UNION BANK, NA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT UNION BANK, NA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication. Denied as moot, in light of the ruling on the Motion for Leave to File an Amended Complaint. (See State Comp. Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131, "Because there is but one complaint in a civil action, the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA REBOLLEDO et al VS. UNION BANK, NA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA REBOLLEDO, HEATHER ALVARADO'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Granted. Plaintiffs may file their proposed First Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this Order. "If the motion to amend is timely made and the granting of the motion will not prejudice the opposing party, it is error to refuse permission to amend and where the refusal also results in a party being deprived of the right to assert a meritorious cause of action or a meritorious defense, it is not only error but an abuse of discretion." (Morgan v. Sup. Ct. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530.) Plaintiffs timely moved to amend the complaint. The prejudice asserted by the Defendant in opposition to this motion can be alleviated by continuing the trial date. The January 12, 2015 trial date is continued to July 13, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ARTI DENTERLEIN VS. JANE O'HARA GAMP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTS JANE GAMP, PAT ALLEY, SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S Motion for Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is granted. The 2007 Student Handbook unambiguously requires a student to maintain a grade point average of at least 70% in his/her fourth year to avoid disqualification. Plaintiff's interpretation of the contract ignores sections 8.2 and 9.0 of the handbook and the court interprets the handbook to give each of its parts effect. See CC 1641. The remaining issues raised by plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint, e.g., whether the grading was fair, plaintiff's interactions with Ms. Gamp and Ms. Alley, etc., do not show arbitrary conduct that warrants court intervention. See Banks v. Dominican College (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 1545, 1551. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LATRELLE GADDIES VS. SAFEWAY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Written Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC. Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Written Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE SUBSTANCE OF THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS MOOT BECAUSE PLAINTIFF LATRELLE GADDIES PROVIDED THE OUTSTANDING VERIFICATIONS AFTER THE MOTION WAS FILED. HOWEVER, SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HER ATTORNEYS IN THE AMOUNT OF $890 PER CRC 3.1348(A). rulings.Case Title: REBECA HERNANDEZ-ARCILLA VS. ARTHUR JACKSON RESIDENTIAL ADULT PROGRAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions; Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF REBECA HERNANDEZ-ARCILLA Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions; Memorandum. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. IT APPEARS FROM THE PAPERS THAT THE MOTIONS HAVE BEEN WITHDRAWN AS TO INTERROGATORIES 3, 4, AND 5, AND DOCUMENT REQUESTS 7, 8, 11, AND 15. AS TO THE DISCOVERY THAT REMAINS IN DISPUTE (REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION 5, 6, 9, 10, 12, AND 13), IT APPEARS THAT AMENDED RESPONSES WERE SUBMITTED AFTER THE INITIAL MOTION WAS FILED. BEFORE THE HEARING, THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER ABOUT THE STATUS OF THE DISCOVERY REMAINING IN DISPUTE AND SHALL APPEAR AT THE HEARING TO DISCUSS THE ISSUES REMAINING IN DISPUTE, THE MERITS OF THEIR POSITIONS, AND THE QUESTION OF SANCTIONS. PARTIES TO BRING PROPOSED ORDERS TO THE HEARING. =(302/JPT)
